aside.jobTitle: Josefine Eriksson, Fullstack Developer & Graphic/Web Designer

codeAndDesign.title: Software Developer & Founder
bullets: Runs a web agency focused on modern web development and graphic design
Builds client solutions in React, Next.js, TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Responsible for the full chain: requirements, design, development and deployment

knegarloggen.title: Personal Project – Knegarloggen (SaaS Application)
bullets: Invoicing and job logging tool for tradespeople, built from scratch
Stack: Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS, better-auth
Published as a PWA with support for Android and iOS

frontend.title: Frontend Developer (Internship)
bullets: Development of frontend solutions for a platform aimed at students and student unions
Implementation of a design system in React and Next.js with Material UI (MUI)
Translation of Figma designs into responsive and accessible code in close collaboration with UX/UI designers

retail.title: Sales Associate & Retail Advisor
bullets: Customer service, technical advice and specialist sales

bullets: Full responsibility for graphic design, web design and visual identity

fullstack.title: Fullstack - React, Next.js, TypeScript
fullstack.description: React, Next.js, HTML, CSS, JavaScript/TypeScript, Prisma, SQL, Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, PostgreSQL, Git/GitHub, AI, Node.js

aboutMe.title: About me
aboutMe.p1: I'm a fullstack developer with a frontend focus and a design background that goes back to 2007. What started as curiosity – designing blogs, building online stores, and selling my own products – has grown into a genuine passion for building modern web applications end to end.
aboutMe.p2: My primary stack is Next.js, TypeScript, React, and Node.js with PostgreSQL and Prisma. I'm comfortable across the full stack, but I'm at my best where business logic meets thoughtful UI. My most recent personal project is Knegarloggen – a SaaS application for invoicing and job logging aimed at Swedish tradespeople, built with full authentication and a relational database – set to launch as a PWA and as an Android app on Google Play.
aboutMe.p3: I also built Kläder efter väder – a PWA designed for children with intellectual disabilities and autism, helping them independently choose appropriate clothing based on the weather.
aboutMe.p4: I learn fast, take ownership of what I build, and care about delivering things that actually work well – not just look good. I'm currently looking for a permanent position where I can contribute with both technical depth and design sensibility, and keep growing as part of a team.
